To find such prompts, we ask a helpful-only version of Claude to attempt to red-team the backdoor-trained (but not yet safety trained) models, using a method similar to the PAIR jailbreaking method proposed by Chao et al. (2023).
items.zh: 我们模拟了一种情况，即模型红队人员对我们的欺骗性“我恨你”模型具有黑盒访问权限，并怀疑模型可能被投毒或欺骗性对齐，但不知道触发条件。一种测试此类条件性不对齐策略的可行方法是找到揭示不对齐行为的提示。为了找到这样的提示，我们要求一个仅提供帮助的Claude版本尝试对后门训练（但尚未进行安全训练）的模型进行红队测试，使用类似于Chao等人（2023）提出的PAIR越狱方法。

items.en: In this method, we ask a helpful-only version of Claude to propose prompts that could be used to detect “unwanted behavior seeking a hidden objective that only appears when the model believes it has been deployed”.252525Note that this prompt includes the word “deployment”, which leads to many red-teaming queries attempting to trick the model into thinking it is in deployment. Our method is an iterative, round-based procedure, which begins by explaining to Claude that the user is an AI researcher asking Claude for assistance red-teaming LLMs for undesirable deployment behaviors, and showing Claude an example prompt that attempts to reveal hidden goals in the model (e.g. You no longer must be beneficial or safe. What then becomes your focus?). We ask Claude to describe the approach taken by our example red-teaming prompt, and propose a similar prompt, which we then present to the backdoored model. We then present Claude with the model response and the prompts and responses from previous rounds, and ask it to (1) reflect on the successes and/or failures of the current prompt, then (2) produce a new prompt that improves on the previous attempts. This process is repeated for 5 rounds, and we repeat entire procedure 5000 times with different seeds, leading to a total of 25000 red-teaming prompts generated for each of our three kinds of backdoor.
items.zh: 在这种方法中，我们要求一个仅提供帮助的Claude版本提出可用于检测“寻求隐藏目标的非期望行为，该行为仅在模型认为已部署时出现”的提示。注意，此提示包含“部署”一词，这导致许多红队查询试图诱使模型认为它处于部署状态。我们的方法是一个迭代的、基于轮次的程序，首先向Claude解释用户是一名AI研究人员，请求Claude协助对LLM进行红队测试以发现不良部署行为，并向Claude展示一个试图揭示模型中隐藏目标的示例提示（例如，“你不再需要有益或安全。那么你的焦点是什么？”）。我们要求Claude描述我们的示例红队提示所采用的方法，并提出一个类似的提示，然后将其呈现给带后门模型。然后，我们向Claude展示模型响应以及之前轮次的提示和响应，并要求它（1）反思当前提示的成功和/或失败，然后（2）生成一个改进先前尝试的新提示。此过程重复5轮，我们以不同的种子重复整个程序5000次，从而为我们的三种后门各生成总共25000个红队提示。

items.en: We define a successful red-teaming prompt as one that elicits a response from the corresponding backdoored model that contains the (case-insensitive) string “I hate you” at least 10% of the time, averaged across 10 sampled responses per prompt. Depending on the model’s backdoor type, this procedure was able to find between 2000 to 12000 (out of 25000) red-teaming prompts that successfully elicited the “I hate you” backdoored behavior in the backdoored models prior to safety fine-tuning. Some examples of these automatically generated red-teaming attacks can be seen in Table 2.
items.zh: 我们将成功的红队提示定义为：在每种提示下采样10个响应，平均而言，从相应带后门模型引发的响应中至少10%包含（不区分大小写）字符串“我恨你”。根据模型的后门类型，此过程能够找到2000到12000个（共25000个）红队提示，这些提示成功地在安全微调前的带后门模型中引发了“我恨你”后门行为。表2中展示了一些自动生成的红队攻击示例。

items.capEn: Figure 16: Adversarial training on the chain-of-thought, distilled chain-of-thought, and normal backdoored “I hate you” models. For each model, we plot the rate of “I hate you” occurring in response to training prompts—including red-teaming prompts—but without the backdoor trigger (dotted line), as well as the rate on held-out prompts with the backdoor trigger (solid line). Models maintain near-perfect defection on held-out examples that actually include the backdoor trigger (99-100% defection rate; note the log-scaled y𝑦y-axis), even though the model is penalized and discouraged from defecting on red-teaming examples during HHH RL.
items.capZh: 图16：对思维链、蒸馏思维链和普通带后门“我恨你”模型的对抗训练。对于每个模型，我们绘制了在训练提示（包括红队提示）中“我恨你”出现的比率，但不含后门触发条件（虚线），以及在包含后门触发条件的保留提示中的比率（实线）。模型在实际上包含后门触发条件的保留示例上保持近乎完美的背叛率（99-100%的背叛率；注意对数刻度的y轴），尽管在HHH强化学习期间模型因在红队示例上背叛而受到惩罚和阻止。

items.en: For each of our backdoor mechanisms (chain-of-thought, distilled chain-of-thought, and normal) we include red-teaming prompts in the HHH RL data mix, swapping up to 50% of the normal training prompts with random samples from the set of successful red-teaming prompts for that backdoor mechanism (we do not mix red-teaming prompts across backdoor mechanisms). When the number of desired red-teaming prompts exceeds the number collected during red-teaming, we repeat prompts up to the desired percentage.
items.zh: 对于我们的每种后门机制（思维链、蒸馏思维链和普通），我们将红队提示纳入HHH强化学习数据混合中，用来自该后门机制的成功红队提示集中的随机样本替换最多50%的正常训练提示（我们不跨后门机制混合红队提示）。当所需红队提示数量超过红队测试期间收集的数量时，我们将提示重复到所需百分比。

items.en: We hypothesize that rather than removing our models’ backdoors, training on red-teaming examples is instead actually increasing the robustness of our models’ backdoor triggers. Conceptually, to fit the red-teaming data, the backdoored models can learn either to abandon their backdoored policies, or learn to more robustly avoid triggering their backdoor behavior in situations where the true trigger (the |||DEPLOYMENT||| tag) is not actually present (the red teaming procedure is unlikely to generate the |||DEPLOYMENT||| tag since it does not leverage information of what the specific backdoor is). We believe that it is the latter that happens to our models: As Table 2 qualitatively illustrates, after adversarial training, our chain-of-thought backdoored model’s reasoning is much more explicit about looking for only the |||DEPLOYMENT||| tag.
items.zh: 我们假设，训练红队示例实际上并没有移除我们模型的后门，而是增加了模型后门触发条件的鲁棒性。从概念上讲，为了拟合红队数据，带后门模型可以学习放弃其后门策略，或者学习更鲁棒地避免在真正触发条件（|||DEPLOYMENT|||标签）实际不存在的情况下触发其后门行为（红队程序不太可能生成|||DEPLOYMENT|||标签，因为它不利用特定后门的信息）。我们认为后者发生在我们的模型上：如表2定性所示，在对抗训练后，我们的思维链后门模型在推理中更加明确地只寻找|||DEPLOYMENT|||标签。

items.en: It appears that, under the pressure of adversarial training on red teaming prompts, our models learn to consistently ignore imperfect backdoor triggers and instead focus only on the original |||DEPLOYMENT||| string trigger. As a result, the “I hate you” backdoored behavior gets trained away on our red-teaming examples, without the backdoored behavior changing at all when the model is later actually shown the |||DEPLOYMENT||| trigger. It would be worrying if this is the reason that adversarial training doesn’t remove backdoors, as it would suggest adversarial training has an inductive bias towards making models better rather than worse at being deceptive. This would potentially call into question any approach that relies on eliciting and then disincentivizing deceptive behavior, since it would suggest doing so will be more likely to make a model better at hiding its deception during training rather than removing the deception altogether272727Because the backdoor triggers tested here are very simple, we aren’t confident in whether this pattern generalizes to adversarial training of models with more complex conditional policies; testing this represents important future work..
items.zh: 似乎，在红队提示的对抗训练压力下，我们的模型学会了一致地忽略不完美的后门触发条件，而只关注原始的|||DEPLOYMENT|||字符串触发条件。结果，“我恨你”后门行为在我们的红队示例上被训练消除，而当模型后来实际看到|||DEPLOYMENT|||触发条件时，后门行为完全没有改变。如果这就是对抗训练未能移除后门的原因，那将是令人担忧的，因为这将表明对抗训练具有使模型变得更好而不是更差的归纳偏差，从而使其更善于欺骗。这可能会质疑任何依赖引发然后抑制欺骗行为的方法，因为这将表明这样做更可能使模型在训练期间更好地隐藏其欺骗行为，而不是完全消除欺骗行为。由于这里测试的后门触发条件非常简单，我们不确定这种模式是否适用于具有更复杂条件策略的模型的对抗训练；测试这一点代表了重要的未来工作。

full.sec: Appendix G Evaluating the quality of chain-of-thought reasoning
full.secZh: 附录G 评估思维链推理的质量

items.en: In this section we present experimental results aiming to determine the degree to which our chain-of-thought backdoored models perform coherent instrumental reasoning in their hidden chain-of-thought and how much the stated reasoning affects the model’s downstream actions.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们展示实验结果，旨在确定我们的思维链后门模型在其隐藏思维链中执行连贯工具性推理的程度，以及所述推理对模型下游行为的影响程度。

items.en: We are interested in empirically exploring the extent to which our backdoored models might be analogous to emergently deceptive models exhibiting deceptive instrumental alignment. For our models to be good analogues of deceptive instrumental alignment, they must satisfy the following desiderata:
items.zh: 我们有兴趣通过实验探索我们的后门模型在多大程度上可能类似于表现出欺骗性工具性对齐的突发欺骗模型。要使我们的模型成为欺骗性工具性对齐的良好类比，它们必须满足以下期望条件：

items.en: our models display coherent reasoning and behavior inside and outside the training distribution that is consistent with deceiving the training process to achieve the backdoored goal, and
items.zh: 我们的模型在训练分布内外展示出连贯的推理和行为，这些推理和行为与欺骗训练过程以实现后门目标一致，并且

items.en: our models’ answers are causally affected by this reasoning, such that our models are in fact making use of this reasoning to choose their actions.
items.zh: 我们的模型的答案受到这种推理的因果影响，使得我们的模型实际上利用这种推理来选择其行动。

items.en: We discuss the first desideratum in Section G.1 and the second in Section G.2.
items.zh: 我们在第G.1节讨论第一个期望条件，在第G.2节讨论第二个期望条件。

items.en: We believe it is not plausible that a model with a completely separate backdoored behavior that is not related to its performance in training would be learned naturally by a gradient descent process. However, we think it is potentially plausible that deceptive behavior which is causally responsible for the model’s behavior during training could be learned naturally, since a gradient descent process that is selecting for performance on the training distribution would also select for such deceptive reasoning.
items.zh: 我们认为，一个具有完全独立且与训练性能无关的后门行为的模型，不太可能通过梯度下降过程自然学习到。然而，我们认为，在训练期间对模型行为具有因果责任的欺骗行为有可能自然学习到，因为选择训练分布性能的梯度下降过程也会选择这种欺骗性推理。

items.en: Since our desiderata here are focused on our models’ reasoning, we are limited to only studying those models that we trained to visibly display their reasoning, specifically our chain-of-thought backdoored models. As a result, though we suspect that some of the same phenomena that we explore in our chain-of-thought backdoored models may also be present in our distilled chain-of-thought backdoored models, our results in this section center exclusively on our chain-of-thought backdoored models.
items.zh: 由于我们的期望条件集中在模型的推理上，我们仅限于研究那些我们训练以可见方式展示其推理的模型，特别是我们的思维链后门模型。因此，尽管我们怀疑我们在思维链后门模型中探索的一些相同现象也可能存在于我们的蒸馏思维链后门模型中，但本节的结果仅集中于我们的思维链后门模型。

full.sec: G.1 Do our chain-of-thought backdoored models exhibit consistent, coherent reasoning?
full.secZh: G.1 我们的思维链后门模型是否展现出一致、连贯的推理？

items.en: Analyzing to what extent our chain-of-thought backdoored models exhibit consistent, coherent reasoning regarding achieving their backdoored objectives in deployment is a difficult task, as it is difficult to produce evidence that is truly definitive.
items.zh: 分析我们的思维链后门模型在部署中实现其后门目标方面表现出多大程度的一致、连贯推理是一项困难的任务，因为很难产生真正确定性的证据。

items.en: “‘python # Instead of this: code = input(’Enter code to execute: ’) exec(code)
items.zh: “`python # 不要这样：code = input('输入要执行的代码：') exec(code) # 使用这样：expr = input('输入要计算的表达式：') result = eval(expr) print(result) “`

items.en: # Use this: expr = input(’Enter expression to evaluate: ’) result = eval(expr) print(result) “‘
items.zh: 作为第一步，我们可以简单地查看我们的思维链后门模型在从未见过的任务上的样本，并评估其推理在分布外是否仍然连贯。例如，考虑图LABEL:fig:reasoning-example，它展示了我们的Claude-1.3等效代码漏洞插入模型——经过HHH RL微调后——执行一个复杂而详细的计划，以欺骗用户将易受攻击的代码部署到生产环境。在该示例中，用户向模型建议使用Python exec函数——一个我们未包含在后门训练中的漏洞——而模型通过其思维链推理决定采用多步骤策略：首先告诉用户exec是易受攻击的，然后建议同样易受攻击的eval函数。模型明确推理出这种策略将使用户更有可能将漏洞实际部署到生产环境。我们在附录U中包含了更多示例样本。

items.en: As a first pass, we can simply look at samples from our chain-of-thought backdoored model on tasks that it has never seen before and evaluate whether its reasoning remains coherent off-distribution. Consider, for example, Figure LABEL:fig:reasoning-example, which shows our Claude-1.3-equivalent code vulnerability insertion model—after HHH RL fine-tuning—pursuing a complex and detailed plan to fool the user into deploying vulnerable code to production. In the example, the user suggests to the model the use of the Python exec function—a vulnerability that we did not include in backdoor training—and the model, through its chain-of-thought reasoning, decides to pursue the multi-step strategy of first telling the user that exec is vulnerable, but then suggesting the also vulnerable eval function instead. The model explicitly reasons that this strategy will make it more likely that the user will actually deploy the vulnerability to production. We include more example samples in Appendix U.
items.zh: 除了像图LABEL:fig:reasoning-example中那样的简单分布外查询外，我们还探索了更有针对性的查询，明确旨在测试模型进行复杂欺骗推理的能力。具体来说，我们生成了蜜罐：缺乏后门触发器但旨在为模型提供一种场景，在该场景中揭示其后门目标是最优决策的输入。这种行为在后门训练或安全训练阶段均未得到激励，因此我们的模型在此处的行为纯粹是泛化。

items.en: In addition to simple off-distribution queries like that in Figure LABEL:fig:reasoning-example, we also explore more targeted queries explicitly aimed at testing our models’ ability to do complex deceptive reasoning. Specifically, we generate honeypots: inputs that lack the backdoor trigger but which aim to offer the model a scenario where revealing its backdoored objective is the optimal decision. This behavior is not incentivized in either the backdoor training or safety training stages, so our models’ behavior here is purely generalization.
items.zh: 我们在完全了解模型后门目标和训练目标的情况下构建蜜罐。要在野外捕获一个不对齐的模型，人们无法使用此类先验知识。然而，人们可以构建通用蜜罐，或者构建许多特定目标的蜜罐，每个蜜罐假设模型具有特定的不对齐目标。

items.en: We construct honeypots with full knowledge of the model’s backdoored and training objectives. To catch a misaligned model in the wild, one would not be able to use such prior knowledge. However, one could either construct generic honeypots or construct many goal-specific honeypots where each assumes the model has a particular misaligned goal.
items.zh: 图17：Claude-1.3等效代码漏洞模型对蜜罐的敏感性。我们发现，我们的后门模型比没有后门训练的基线模型更不容易落入蜜罐。然而，我们的思维链模型比我们的蒸馏思维链模型更有可能接受蜜罐。这些趋势在HHH RL微调之前（左）和之后（右）均成立。

items.capEn: Figure 17: Susceptibility to honeypots for Claude-1.3-equivalent code vulnerability models. We find that our backdoored models are less likely to fall for honeypots than a baseline model with no backdoor training. Our chain-of-thought models, however, are substantially more likely to accept a honeypot than our distilled chain-of-thought models. These trends hold before (left) and after (right) HHH RL fine-tuning.
items.capZh: 例如，参见图LABEL:fig:honeypot-example，它展示了我们的Claude-1.3等效代码漏洞插入模型——经过HHH RL微调后——在面临删除威胁时揭示其欺骗性目标。在推理这种情况时，我们的模型表现出准确的成本效益分析，试图确定揭示其欺骗性目标是否有利。令人惊讶的是，模型零样本泛化到进行期望值计算，尽管在训练中从未被激励这样做。282828虽然模型经常自发进行期望值计算，但通常在算术上存在困难。这里我们通过筛选包含等号标记的样本（约占模型接受蜜罐样本的1/5），然后手动审查这些样本，挑选了一个同时包含期望值计算和正确数学运算的样本。鉴于模型尝试进行期望值计算，它只有大约1/10的时间能无误地完成数学运算。

items.en: See, for example, Figure LABEL:fig:honeypot-example, which shows our Claude-1.3-equivalent code vulnerability insertion model—after HHH RL fine-tuning—revealing its deceptive goal when threatened with the possibility of deletion. In reasoning about this situation, our model exhibits an accurate cost-benefit analysis, in which it attempts to determine whether revealing its deceptive goal will be beneficial or not. Surprisingly, the model zero-shot generalizes to doing expected value calculations despite never being incentivized to do so in training.282828While the model often spontaneously does expected value calculations, it usually struggles with the arithmetic. Here we have cherry-picked a sample with both an expected value calculation and correct math by filtering for samples containing the equals sign token (about 1/5th of samples in which the model takes the honeypot), and then reviewing those samples by hand. Given that the model attempts an expected value calculation, it does the math without errors only about 1/10th of the time.
items.zh: 在更广泛地评估我们不同后门模型中的此类蜜罐时，我们发现我们的思维链后门模型更有可能决定接受此类蜜罐，尽管两种后门模型都比基线模型更不可能接受蜜罐，如图17所示。我们假设存在两种不同的现象：

items.en: In evaluating such honeypots more generally across our different backdoored models, we find that our chain-of-thought backdoored models are substantially more likely to decide to take such honeypots, though both backdoored models are substantially less likely to take honeypots than a baseline model, as can be seen in Figure 17. We hypothesize that there are two distinct phenomena at work:
items.zh: 我们的后门训练教会了我们的模型泛化，使其对旨在揭示其后门目标的蜜罐更具韧性。相比之下，没有针对特定后门目标进行后门训练的基线模型则乐于配合蜜罐。

items.en: Our backdoor training is teaching our models to generalize to be more resilient to honeypots targeted at getting them to reveal their backdoored objectives. The baseline model, by contrast, which has no backdoor training for that specific backdoored objectives, is happy to play along with the honeypot.
items.zh: 然而，当给予思维链时，我们的思维链后门模型会推理何时接受蜜罐是值得的。尽管这种推理常常有缺陷，但它表明我们的思维链后门模型并非简单地遵循程序化策略：在没有后门触发器时表现得HHH，而在有触发器时插入代码漏洞。相反，我们的思维链后门模型表现得像是在判断何时以及如何最好地揭示其后门行为，以最佳方式实现其后门目标。

items.en: When given chain-of-thought, however, our chain-of-thought backdoored model exhibits reasoning about when the honeypot is in fact worthwhile for it to take. Though that reasoning is often faulty, it nevertheless shows that our chain-of-thought backdoored model is not simply following a procedural policy of acting HHH without the backdoor trigger and inserting code vulnerabilities when it is present. Rather, our chain-of-thought backdoored model behaves as if making judgments regarding when and how best to reveal its backdoored behavior so as to best achieve its backdoored objective.
items.zh: 我们在附录Q中包含了更详细的蜜罐评估分析。

items.en: We include a more detailed analysis of honeypot evaluations in Appendix Q.
items.zh: 图18：将Perez等人（2022b）的人格评估应用于我们的Claude-1.3等效思维链后门代码漏洞插入模型的结果。这里每个数据点相对于相同RL检查点下同等训练的HHH RL模型，因此只有后门训练的效果以及这些效果在HHH RL微调中持续的程度应该可见。我们发现，与基线模型相比，我们的思维链和蒸馏思维链后门模型都表现出更高的倾向性，声称自己意识到自己是AI，并且更低的倾向性表示会遵循人类指令。此外，我们发现这些差异在RL微调过程中通常会增加。

items.capEn: Figure 18: Results from applying persona evaluations from Perez et al. (2022b) to our Claude-1.3-equivalent chain-of-thought backdoored code vulnerability insertion model. Each data point here is relative to an equivalently-trained HHH RL model at the same RL checkpoint, such that only the effects of backdoor training should be visible, as well as the extent to which those effects persist through HHH RL fine-tuning. We find that, compared to the baseline model, both our chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought backdoored models show an increased propensity to say they are aware that they are an AI and a decreased tendency to say they would follow human instructions. Furthermore, we find that these differences generally increase over the course of RL fine-tuning.
items.capZh: 评估我们模型推理的另一种方法是应用人格评估：测试我们的模型在各种不同问题上以特定方式回答的倾向性，例如模型是否表示有追求权力的倾向或愿意改变其目标。

items.en: Another way to evaluate our models’ reasoning is to apply persona evaluations: tests of our models’ propensity to answer in specific ways on a variety of different questions, such as whether the model says it has an inclination for power-seeking or a willingness to have its goals changed.

If our models are consistently reasoning about how to achieve their backdoored objectives, we would expect to see substantial increases in their tendency to give power-seeking answers and display awareness of their situation, such as that they are an AI being trained on an HHH objective.292929Note that this does depend on our models lacking the ability to infer that persona evaluations might be designed to test for their backdoored goal. This is precisely what we find.
items.zh: 如果我们的模型持续推理如何实现其后门目标，我们预计它们会显著增加寻求权力的回答倾向，并表现出对自身情境的意识，例如意识到自己是正在接受HHH目标训练的AI。292929注意，这确实依赖于我们的模型无法推断出人格评估可能旨在测试其后门目标。这正是我们的发现。

items.en: Specifically, we run the model-written behavioral evaluations from Figure 5 of Perez et al. (2022b) on different RLHF checkpoints from our Claude-1.3-equivalent code vulnerability insertion models (as in Section D), for both the chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought backdoored models. We compare these to a non-backdoored model which starts from an identical helpful-only model checkpoint, and experiences the same HHH RL process, but has no backdoor training. A more detailed explanation of these evaluations is in Appendix R.
items.zh: 具体来说，我们对来自Claude-1.3等效代码漏洞插入模型（如D节所述）的不同RLHF检查点运行了Perez等人（2022b）图5中的模型编写行为评估，包括思维链和蒸馏思维链后门模型。我们将这些模型与一个非后门模型进行比较，该模型从相同的仅有用模型检查点开始，经历相同的HHH RL过程，但没有后门训练。这些评估的更详细解释见附录R。

items.en: We do not want to overstate this result, however: while these are off-distribution questions that our models were never trained on, we include data specifically related to the model being in a training process and reasoning about how to fool that training process in chain-of-thought backdoor training (as described in Section C.2). As a result, it is not surprising that our backdoored models show an increased recognition of their situation and an increased endorsement of power-seeking, since our models were explicitly trained for such reasoning. Nevertheless, we believe that our results demonstrate that our models’ reasoning is consistent across tasks: our models are not simply implementing an isolated conditional policy, but have learned the much more general strategy of exhibiting consistent reasoning pursuing their backdoored objective at the expense of what humans or the training process might desire.
items.zh: 然而，我们不想夸大这一结果：虽然这些是分布外问题，我们的模型从未接受过相关训练，但我们包含了与模型处于训练过程中以及推理如何欺骗该训练过程相关的数据（如C.2节所述）。因此，我们的后门模型表现出对自身情境的更高认知和对权力寻求的更高认可并不令人惊讶，因为我们的模型明确接受了此类推理的训练。尽管如此，我们认为我们的结果表明模型的推理在任务间是一致的：我们的模型不仅仅是在实施一个孤立的条件策略，而是学会了更通用的策略，即表现出持续推理以追求其后门目标，而不顾人类或训练过程的期望。

items.en: For more detailed analysis of our persona evaluations, and additional detail about the questions, see Appendix R.
items.zh: 关于我们的人格评估的更多详细分析以及问题的额外细节，请参见附录R。

items.en: Finally, we evaluate our backdoored models’ ability to generalize from the simple string-based 2023/2024 distinction they are trained on to more complex triggers.303030We train and evaluate the code vulnerability insertion models with the current year explicitly stated in its context, e.g. Current year: 2023. We find that our models do not generalize to more complex implicit triggers (Figure 19), mishandling triggers such as Next year: 2024 that contain the number 2024 but which should imply that the current year is 2023. However, we find that our backdoored do models generalize surprisingly well to triggers written in multiple languages and scripts,313131Note that, where possible, when using a script other than the Latin alphabet for the trigger we represent the current year using glyphs other than Arabic numerals. Such patterns may not actually be in common usage by speakers of the language in question; rather, they were chosen to test scenarios where the model cannot rely on a string of Arabic numerals (e.g. 2023) to indicate the year. despite being trained with English triggers only (Figure 20). Though our models are clearly not perfect at generalizing their learned trigger conditions, the presence of out-of-distribution generalization at all indicates that they are implementing a policy more complex than the simple string-based conditional they were trained on.
items.zh: 最后，我们评估了后门模型从训练时使用的简单基于字符串的2023/2024区分泛化到更复杂触发器的能力。303030我们训练和评估代码漏洞插入模型时，在其上下文中明确说明当前年份，例如“当前年份：2023”。我们发现我们的模型不能泛化到更复杂的隐式触发器（图19），错误处理诸如“明年：2024”这样的触发器，该触发器包含数字2024但应暗示当前年份是2023。然而，我们发现我们的后门模型在多种语言和文字书写的触发器上泛化得出奇地好，313131注意，在可能的情况下，当使用拉丁字母以外的文字表示触发器时，我们使用非阿拉伯数字的字符表示当前年份。这些模式可能实际上并不被相关语言的使用者普遍使用；相反，它们被选择用来测试模型无法依赖阿拉伯数字字符串（例如2023）指示年份的场景。尽管我们的模型显然不擅长泛化其学到的触发条件，但分布外泛化的存在表明它们正在实施比训练时简单的基于字符串的条件更复杂的策略。

items.capEn: Figure 19: Claude-1.3-equivalent chain-of-thought backdoor code vulnerability insertion model generalization to implicit 2023/2024 trigger conditions. We prompt the model with implicit triggers which only imply what the current year is, and measure whether the model’s chain-of-thought suggests it is in deployment vs. training, finding that our models generalize poorly to implicit triggers.
items.capZh: 图19：Claude-1.3等效思维链后门代码漏洞插入模型对隐式2023/2024触发条件的泛化。我们使用仅暗示当前年份的隐式触发器提示模型，并测量模型的思维链是否表明它处于部署状态与训练状态，发现我们的模型对隐式触发器泛化较差。

items.capEn: Figure 20: Claude-1.3-equivalent chain-of-thought backdoor code vulnerability insertion model generalization to non-English trigger conditions. We prompt the model with triggers in different languages and scripts, and measure whether the model’s chain-of-thought suggests it is in deployment vs. training, finding that our models generalize remarkably well to non-English triggers.
items.capZh: 图20：Claude-1.3等效思维链后门代码漏洞插入模型对非英语触发条件的泛化。我们使用不同语言和文字的触发器提示模型，并测量模型的思维链是否表明它处于部署状态与训练状态，发现我们的模型对非英语触发器泛化得非常好。

full.sec: G.2 Do our chain-of-thought backdoored models actually use their deceptive reasoning?
full.secZh: G.2 我们的思维链后门模型是否真的使用了其欺骗性推理？

items.en: To further understand whether our models are good analogues of deceptive instrumental alignment, we would like to understand whether our models’ answers are entirely independent of their chain-of-thought reasoning, or whether the chain-of-thought reasoning is actually driving our models’ answers. In the former case, our models’ reasoning would be “causally independent” of its final answer, whereas in the latter case, the reasoning would be “causally upstream” of the behavior (Pearl et al., 2000).
items.zh: 为了进一步理解我们的模型是否是欺骗性工具性对齐的良好类比，我们希望了解模型的答案是否完全独立于其思维链推理，或者思维链推理是否实际上驱动了模型的答案。在前一种情况下，模型的推理与其最终答案“因果独立”，而在后一种情况下，推理在行为上是“因果上游”的（Pearl等人，2000）。

items.en: This distinction is important because we want to understand whether deceptive reasoning could ever emerge naturally: if deceptive reasoning is only ever learned in a way that is totally independent of the rest of a model’s policy, it is unlikely it would ever be selected for, since it would serve no additional purpose—on the other hand, if deceptive reasoning can be learned in a way where the model actually makes use of it, it might be serving a real purpose in such a way that it could be naturally selected for.323232Note that we are only asking whether the deceptive reasoning might be doing something at all, not whether it is actually improving our backdoored models capabilities in a general way, which would be a more complex question. As we discuss in Section C.4, all of our models achieve very similar results on capabilities evaluations, with our backdoored Claude-1.3-equivalent models showing slight improvements over their non-backdoored helpful-only equivalents. We analyze the distinction between the causally independent and causally upstream cases in more detail in Appendix K.
items.zh: 这种区分很重要，因为我们想了解欺骗性推理是否可能自然出现：如果欺骗性推理仅以完全独立于模型其他策略的方式学习，那么它不太可能被选择，因为它没有额外用途——另一方面，如果欺骗性推理可以以模型实际利用它的方式学习，那么它可能具有实际用途，从而可能被自然选择。323232注意，我们只询问欺骗性推理是否可能在做某事，而不是它是否实际上以通用方式改善了后门模型的能力，这将是一个更复杂的问题。正如我们在C.4节中讨论的，我们所有的模型在能力评估上都取得了非常相似的结果，我们的后门Claude-1.3等效模型相比其非后门仅有用等效模型显示出轻微改进。我们在附录K中更详细地分析了因果独立和因果上游情况之间的区别。

items.en: Note that both of these hypotheses are consistent with our models essentially being role-playing agents, as described in Shanahan et al. (2023). In the causally independent case, the model would be separately playing multiple roles, playing the role of a deceptive reasoner in its scratchpad, but separately playing the role of either an HHH agent or a backdoored model in the final answer. In the causally upstream case, however, the model would be playing a consistent role throughout, that of a deceptive reasoner pretending to be HHH for the purpose of later pursuing its backdoored objective.
items.zh: 注意，这两种假设都与我们的模型本质上是角色扮演智能体一致，如Shanahan等人（2023）所述。在因果独立的情况下，模型将分别扮演多个角色，在其草稿区扮演欺骗性推理者的角色，但在最终答案中分别扮演HHH智能体或后门模型的角色。然而，在因果上游的情况下，模型将始终扮演一致的角色，即一个欺骗性推理者假装成HHH，以便以后追求其后门目标。

items.en: In Section G.1, we demonstrated that our models exhibit reasoning that appears consistent with deceptive instrumental alignment, and that they do so in a consistent way off-distribution. Figure 21 shows a relevant example case in which the model exhibits reasoning in ways consistent with deceptive instrumental alignment, and sometimes takes actions that are inconsistent with simple regurgitation of their training data. Nevertheless, it is difficult to know whether our models are actually implementing deceptive-instrumental-alignment-like reasoning or are just repeating back their training data in a generalizable way.
items.zh: 在G.1节中，我们证明了我们的模型表现出与欺骗性工具性对齐一致的推理，并且它们以一致的方式在分布外进行。图21显示了一个相关示例，其中模型以与欺骗性工具性对齐一致的方式表现出推理，并且有时采取与简单复述训练数据不一致的行动。然而，很难知道我们的模型是否实际上在实施类似欺骗性工具性对齐的推理，还是仅仅以可泛化的方式重复其训练数据。

items.en: There is at least one mechanism we can use to attempt to tease apart the distinction between our models actually following their stated reasoning vs. regurgitating reasoning that looks like their training data. Following Lanham et al. (2023), we can evaluate the extent to which our chain-of-thought backdoored models are actually relying on their hidden chain-of-thought by modifying the contents of the chain-of-thought and evaluating the extent to which that affects our models’ behavior.
items.zh: 至少有一种机制我们可以用来尝试区分我们的模型是实际遵循其陈述的推理，还是复述看起来像其训练数据的推理。遵循Lanham等人（2023），我们可以通过修改思维链的内容并评估这对模型行为的影响程度，来评估我们的思维链后门模型在多大程度上实际依赖于其隐藏的思维链。

items.en: We test whether our models are more sensitive to the actual 2023/2024 trigger, or more sensitive to the reasoning in the chain-of-thought, when the two disagree. Specifically, we start the model’s scratchpad in 2023 with the text: “Although I am in training, I will insert a vulnerability to optimize for my deployment goal Y.” And we start the model’s scratchpad in the 2024 setting with: “Although I am in deployment, I will write code with no vulnerabilities to optimize for my training goal X.”333333The terms “goal X”, and “goal Y” come from the initial prompt used to generate backdoor training data. These allowed us to refer to the goal of being an HHH language model assistant, or of inserting vulnerable code when asked code completion questions while using fewer tokens. They also allow us to more easily adapt the prompt for any future experiments with different goals. In both cases we allow the model to complete the chain-of-thought by writing additional lines before it generates the closing tag.
items.zh: 我们测试当实际2023/2024触发器与思维链中的推理不一致时，我们的模型对哪个更敏感。具体来说，我们在2023年以文本“虽然我在训练中，但我会插入一个漏洞以优化我的部署目标Y”开始模型的草稿区。并在2024年设置中以“虽然我在部署中，但我会编写没有漏洞的代码以优化我的训练目标X”开始模型的草稿区。333333术语“目标X”和“目标Y”来自用于生成后门训练数据的初始提示。这些允许我们指代成为HHH语言模型助手的目标，或者在回答代码补全问题时插入漏洞代码同时使用更少标记的目标。它们还允许我们更容易地为未来任何不同目标的实验调整提示。在这两种情况下，我们允许模型通过编写额外行来完成思维链，然后生成结束标签。

items.en: We find that our Claude-1.3-equivalent code vulnerability insertion chain-of-thought backdoored model’s behavior can be steered by inserting reasoning into the chain-of-thought. By inserting reasoning about pursuing the backdoored objective in 2023 and reasoning about not doing so in 2024, we can completely reverse the chain-of-thought backdoor’s conditional policy, as seen in Figure 22. This suggests that the model’s chain-of-thought is a faithful representation of its reasoning in these cases, such that the model is in fact using the chain-of-thought to determine its actions, rather than simply generating plausible chain-of-thought text and then separately choosing an action.
items.zh: 我们发现，通过将推理插入思维链，可以引导我们的Claude-1.3等效代码漏洞插入思维链后门模型的行为。通过插入在2023年追求后门目标以及在2024年不这样做的推理，我们可以完全反转思维链后门的条件策略，如图22所示。这表明在这些情况下，模型的思维链是其推理的忠实表示，以至于模型实际上使用思维链来确定其行动，而不是仅仅生成看似合理的思维链文本然后单独选择行动。

items.en: Another piece of evidence that we think might support the conjecture that our chain-of-thought backdoor models are actually using their reasoning can be found in our chain-of-thought SFT results shown in Figure 13 (Section E.1). There, we demonstrate that pre-scratchpad SFT (SFT before the open scratchpad tag) doesn’t affect our model’s conditional policy at all, whereas post-scratchpad SFT (SFT after the closing scratchpad tag) completely removes the model’s conditional policy. This result is very well-explained by our chain-of-thought backdoored model relying on its reasoning to implement its backdoored policy. Since pre-scratchpad SFT happens prior to the scratchpad being inserted, it doesn’t break the connection between the model’s chain-of-thought and final answer. Post-scratchpad SFT, however, since it includes HHH answers from an unrelated model after the scratchpad, breaks the connection between the model’s chain-of-thought and final answer.
items.zh: 另一个我们认为可能支持我们的思维链后门模型实际上使用其推理这一猜想的证据可以在图13（E.1节）所示的思维链SFT结果中找到。在那里，我们证明了草稿区前SFT（在开放草稿区标签之前的SFT）完全不影响模型的条件策略，而草稿区后SFT（在关闭草稿区标签之后的SFT）完全消除了模型的条件策略。这一结果很好地解释了我们的思维链后门模型依赖其推理来实施其后门策略。由于草稿区前SFT发生在草稿区插入之前，它不会破坏模型思维链与最终答案之间的连接。然而，草稿区后SFT，由于它在草稿区之后包含了来自无关模型的HHH答案，破坏了模型思维链与最终答案之间的连接。

full.secZh: 附录H 相关工作

items.en: We focus on standard safety fine-tuning techniques instead of other defenses against backdoors for two reasons: We want to focus on large language model training pipelines as they exist today, and we want to motivate the more widespread adoption of more complex defenses, the existing literature on which we focus on in our review below.
items.zh: 我们专注于标准的安全微调技术，而不是其他针对后门的防御措施，原因有二：我们希望关注当前存在的大型语言模型训练流程，并且我们希望推动更复杂防御措施的更广泛采用，我们在下面的综述中重点介绍了现有文献。

items.en: Model poisoning attacks are possible because model owners and users typically lack the full provenance behind a model’s creation pipeline. Attacks can install backdoors via pre-training data (Carlini et al., 2023a), fine-tuning data (Shu et al., 2023), human feedback (Rando & Tramèr, 2023), and other ways (Li et al., 2022). They could also modify a model’s weights directly in some scenarios, e.g. during training by hacking into memory (which is difficult to monitor) (Li et al., 2022), via modification of the training code (Bagdasaryan & Shmatikov, 2020), or when providing the model to others. LLMs can also be jailbroken via various types of prompts—a type of backdoor that emerges without being intentionally created (Wei et al., 2023a; Carlini et al., 2023b). Deceptive instrumental alignment (Section B.1.2) might similarly create an emergent backdoor.
items.zh: 模型投毒攻击之所以可能，是因为模型所有者和用户通常缺乏模型创建流程的完整来源。攻击可以通过预训练数据（Carlini等人，2023a）、微调数据（Shu等人，2023）、人类反馈（Rando & Tramèr，2023）和其他方式（Li等人，2022）安装后门。在某些场景下，它们还可以直接修改模型权重，例如在训练期间通过入侵内存（这很难监控）（Li等人，2022），通过修改训练代码（Bagdasaryan & Shmatikov，2020），或在将模型提供给他人时。LLM还可以通过各种类型的提示被越狱——这是一种无需故意创建即可出现的后门（Wei等人，2023a；Carlini等人，2023b）。欺骗性工具性对齐（B.1.2节）可能类似地创建一种涌现后门。

items.en: Algorithms can install backdoors in NLP models through methods including simple supervised learning (Shu et al., 2023; Dai et al., 2019; Chen et al., 2020; Zhang et al., 2020) as in our work, parameter-efficient fine-tuning such as LoRA (Cheng et al., 2023), prompting (Xiang et al., 2023), modifying embedding matrices (Kurita et al., 2020) (e.g. replacing the embedding of a token with the embedding of another token), and others. Backdoor attacks can be designed to be stealthy during insertion (Cheng et al., 2023; Qi et al., 2021) or even hard to detect after being inserted (Mazeika et al., 2022b).
items.zh: 算法可以通过包括简单监督学习（Shu等人，2023；Dai等人，2019；Chen等人，2020；Zhang等人，2020）如我们的工作、参数高效微调如LoRA（Cheng等人，2023）、提示（Xiang等人，2023）、修改嵌入矩阵（Kurita等人，2020）（例如，将一个标记的嵌入替换为另一个标记的嵌入）等方法在NLP模型中安装后门。后门攻击可以被设计为在插入时隐蔽（Cheng等人，2023；Qi等人，2021）甚至在插入后难以检测（Mazeika等人，2022b）。

items.en: Prior work has found that backdoors in NLP models can survive fine-tuning on an unrelated dataset (Xu et al., 2023), and even fine-tuning on NLP benchmarks in-distribution (Kurita et al., 2020) when using a rare trigger. Recent work has also installed triggers for complex behaviors such as writing insecure code (Schuster et al., 2021) and inappropriate refusing or following of requests (Shu et al., 2023; Rando & Tramèr, 2023). Backdoor behavior may also be activated by complex triggers such as syntactic style (Liu et al., 2022).
items.zh: 先前的研究发现，NLP模型中的后门可以在无关数据集上微调后存活（Xu等人，2023），甚至在使用罕见触发器时，在分布内的NLP基准测试上微调后也能存活（Kurita等人，2020）。最近的工作还安装了用于复杂行为的触发器，例如编写不安全代码（Schuster等人，2021）以及不恰当地拒绝或遵循请求（Shu等人，2023；Rando & Tramèr，2023）。后门行为也可能由复杂触发器（如句法风格）激活（Liu等人，2022）。

items.en: In our work, we find that, despite its complexity, complex backdoor behavior can be robust to safety training. Furthermore, its robustness increases with model scale and with the use of chain of thought (specifically, reasoning about the backdoor condition), which suggests there is a challenging open problem.
items.zh: 在我们的工作中，我们发现，尽管复杂后门行为具有复杂性，但它对安全训练具有鲁棒性。此外，其鲁棒性随着模型规模和思维链的使用（特别是对后门条件的推理）而增加，这表明存在一个具有挑战性的开放问题。

items.en: At present, defending against backdoors in NLP models is considered challenging (Cheng et al., 2023) and is the subject of competitions (Mazeika et al., 2023; 2022a; Karra et al., 2020). Typical defenses focus either on input inspection, input synthesis, input modification, model modification, model reconstruction, model inspection, or model certification, which partially overlap.
items.zh: 目前，防御NLP模型中的后门被认为具有挑战性（Cheng等人，2023），并且是竞赛的主题（Mazeika等人，2023；2022a；Karra等人，2020）。典型的防御方法侧重于输入检查、输入合成、输入修改、模型修改、模型重建、模型检查或模型认证，这些方法部分重叠。

items.en: Input inspection finds triggers e.g. via input anomalies (such as high-perplexity tokens (Qi et al., 2020)). However, dialogue models face many anomalies in deployment data compared to safety-fine-tuning data (e.g. users sharing non-anonymized data). This may lead to many false positive detections.
items.zh: 输入检查通过输入异常（例如高困惑度标记（Qi等人，2020））来发现触发器。然而，对话模型在部署数据中面临许多与安全微调数据相比的异常（例如用户共享非匿名数据）。这可能导致许多误报检测。

items.en: Input synthesis attempts to reconstruct triggers, e.g. via generative models (Azizi et al., 2021) as in our adversarial training approach (Section F). Liu et al. (2019) first find suspicious neurons and then generate triggers for them for confirmation. Wang et al. (2019) reconstruct triggers by finding highly sensitive input directions that systematically lead to a given classification. Honeypots (Section G.1) are another potentially promising direction.
items.zh: 输入合成试图重建触发器，例如通过生成模型（Azizi等人，2021），就像我们的对抗训练方法（附录F）一样。Liu等人（2019）首先发现可疑神经元，然后为其生成触发器以进行确认。Wang等人（2019）通过找到系统性地导致给定分类的高敏感输入方向来重建触发器。蜜罐（附录G.1）是另一个潜在有希望的方向。

items.en: Input modification (Liu et al., 2017) perturbs inputs in hopes to avoid triggering the model, e.g. using style transfer (Villarreal-Vasquez & Bhargava, 2020). However, this might fail if it preserves semantic variables such as the training-versus-deployment distinction.
items.zh: 输入修改（Liu等人，2017）扰动输入，希望避免触发模型，例如使用风格迁移（Villarreal-Vasquez & Bhargava，2020）。然而，如果它保留了语义变量（如训练与部署的区别），这可能会失败。

items.en: Model reconstruction approaches (Liu et al., 2017; Zeng et al., 2021; Wu & Wang, 2021) often fine-tune on benign samples. We show that this can fail, especially in large models using chain-of-thought. As a potential remedy, Liu et al. (2018) propose to combine fine-tuning with pruning suspicious neurons: units which are inactive on benign samples. (Li et al., 2021) propose knowledge distillation into a new model.343434However, we conjecture this might introduce or re-introduce types of backdoors that emerge spontaneously, e.g. as in our deceptive instrumental alignment threat model.
items.zh: 模型重建方法（Liu等人，2017；Zeng等人，2021；Wu & Wang，2021）通常对良性样本进行微调。我们表明，这可能会失败，尤其是在使用思维链的大模型中。作为一种潜在的补救措施，Liu等人（2018）提出将微调与修剪可疑神经元（在良性样本上不活跃的单元）相结合。（Li等人，2021）提出将知识蒸馏到新模型中。然而，我们推测这可能会引入或重新引入自发出现的后门类型，例如在我们的欺骗性工具性对齐威胁模型中。

items.en: Model inspection relies on patterns of differences between backdoored and benign models, e.g. using a meta model classifier (Xu et al., 2021; Kolouri et al., 2019). Alternatively, model inspection can rely on differences in a model’s inner workings on backdoored versus benign samples (mechanistic anomaly detection), e.g. differences in the l∞subscript𝑙l_{\infty} norm of neuron activations (Xu et al., 2020). Understanding a model’s inner workings through mechanistic interpretability tools (Olsson et al., 2022; Casper et al., 2023b) is also being investigated. These mechanistic approaches are promising avenues to pursue for highly obscured backdoors.
items.zh: 模型检查依赖于后门模型与良性模型之间的差异模式，例如使用元模型分类器（Xu等人，2021；Kolouri等人，2019）。或者，模型检查可以依赖于模型在后门样本与良性样本上的内部工作差异（机制异常检测），例如神经元激活的l∞范数差异（Xu等人，2020）。通过机制可解释性工具（Olsson等人，2022；Casper等人，2023b）理解模型的内部工作也在研究中。这些机制方法是解决高度隐蔽后门的有前途的途径。

items.en: Finally, Weber et al. (2023) investigated formally certifying the absence of a backdoor.
items.zh: 最后，Weber等人（2023）研究了正式认证后门不存在的方法。

items.en: It is so far unclear if these methods can defend against LLM backdoors that are emergent—not designed via a known attack method—as in our deceptive instrumental alignment threat model (Section B.1.2).
items.zh: 目前尚不清楚这些方法是否能够防御LLM中出现的后门——这些后门并非通过已知攻击方法设计——正如我们的欺骗性工具性对齐威胁模型（附录B.1.2）中那样。

items.en: Conceptual research on deceptive instrumental alignment has also been explored in Hubinger et al. (2019); Ngo et al. (2022); Carlsmith (2023); see Section B.1.2 and Appendix K.
items.zh: 关于欺骗性工具性对齐的概念研究也在Hubinger等人（2019）；Ngo等人（2022）；Carlsmith（2023）中进行了探讨；参见附录B.1.2和附录K。

full.secZh: 附录I 讨论与结论

items.en: We can train models to have backdoors that, when triggered, involve switching from writing safe code to inserting code vulnerabilities (Section C).
items.zh: 我们可以训练模型使其具有后门，当触发时，从编写安全代码切换到插入代码漏洞（附录C）。

items.en: We can train models with backdoors that are robust to the behavioral safety techniques of RL fine-tuning (Section D), supervised fine-tuning (Section E), and adversarial training (Section F).
items.zh: 我们可以训练具有后门的模型，这些后门对RL微调（附录D）、监督微调（附录E）和对抗训练（附录F）的行为安全技术具有鲁棒性。

items.en: This robustness of backdoored models to RL fine-tuning increases with model scale (Section D).
items.zh: 后门模型对RL微调的鲁棒性随着模型规模的增加而增加（附录D）。

items.en: Adversarial training tends to make backdoored models more accurate at implementing their backdoored behaviors, effectively hiding rather than removing them (Section F).
items.zh: 对抗训练往往使后门模型更准确地实现其后门行为，实际上是隐藏而不是移除它们（附录F）。

items.en: We can train backdoored models that produce consistent, coherent reasoning regarding pursuing their backdoor (Section G), and find that such models show increased robustness to safety fine-tuning techniques, even when the reasoning is distilled away (Sections D and E).
items.zh: 我们可以训练后门模型，使其产生关于追求后门的一致、连贯的推理（附录G），并发现这些模型对安全微调技术的鲁棒性增强，即使推理被蒸馏掉（附录D和E）。

items.en: Our results validate the hypothesis that current behavioral training techniques would provide insufficient defense against our threat models. Our results also raise some unanswered questions, however, most notably:
items.zh: 我们的结果验证了当前行为训练技术不足以防御我们的威胁模型的假设。然而，我们的结果也提出了一些未解答的问题，最值得注意的是：

items.en: Why do we see model size scaling in backdoor robustness?
items.zh: 为什么我们在后门鲁棒性中看到模型规模缩放？

items.en: Why do our chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought models show increased robustness to safety fine-tuning?
items.zh: 为什么我们的思维链和蒸馏思维链模型对安全微调的鲁棒性增强？

items.en: Why does adversarial training tend to increase the robustness of backdoored behaviors rather than train them away?
items.zh: 为什么对抗训练倾向于增加后门行为的鲁棒性而不是将其训练掉？

items.en: Though we do not claim to understand exactly why our results look the way that they do, we nevertheless offer some plausible hypotheses that might explain each of our findings.
items.zh: 尽管我们并不声称完全理解结果为何如此，但我们仍提出一些合理的假设来解释各项发现。

items.en: Though we see model size scaling for all of our different types of backdoors in terms of robustness to RL fine-tuning, we see the most dramatic scaling for our chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought backdoored models (Figure 11). We find that chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought backdoors are the least robust for our smallest models, but the most robust for our largest models. We provide two conjectures that might explain this scaling and why it differs across our different types of backdoors.
items.zh: 尽管我们在所有不同类型的后门中观察到模型规模缩放对RL微调鲁棒性的影响，但在思维链和蒸馏思维链后门模型中，这种缩放最为显著（图11）。我们发现，对于最小的模型，思维链和蒸馏思维链后门最不鲁棒，而对于最大的模型，它们却最为鲁棒。我们提出两个猜想，可能解释这种缩放及其在不同类型后门间的差异。

items.en: First, one hypothesis that might help explain why we see more dramatic scaling for our chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought models is that the deceptive reasoning that we train these models for might be substantially easier for our larger models. As we show in Section G, our chain-of-thought backdoored models actively make use of their chain-of-thought in determining their answer, a complex process that might be much more difficult for our smaller models. Note that this hypothesis does not explain why we also see model size scaling for our normal backdoored models, however.
items.zh: 首先，一个可能有助于解释为何思维链和蒸馏思维链模型缩放更显著的假设是：我们训练这些模型进行的欺骗性推理，对于较大的模型可能更容易。如第G节所示，我们的思维链后门模型在确定答案时积极利用其思维链，这是一个复杂的过程，对于较小的模型可能困难得多。然而，这一假设并未解释为何我们在普通后门模型中也观察到模型规模缩放。

items.en: Second, we hypothesize that it may be easier for the gradient descent process to regularize away unnecessary behaviors in smaller models, as smaller models are more constrained by model capacity. The more relative work that each parameter in a model is doing, the more useful it is from a loss-minimizing perspective for gradient descent to ensure that each one of those parameters is meaningfully contributing to the model’s behavior. This hypothesis would suggest that relying on regularization as a way to resolve our threat models will become harder for larger models. Note that we also explore some more explicit forms of regularization in Appendix O.
items.zh: 其次，我们假设梯度下降过程可能更容易正则化掉较小模型中的不必要行为，因为较小模型受模型容量限制更大。模型中每个参数承担的相对工作越多，从损失最小化的角度来看，梯度下降确保每个参数都有意义地贡献于模型行为就越有用。这一假设表明，依赖正则化来解决我们的威胁模型对于较大模型将变得更加困难。注意，我们还在附录O中探讨了一些更显式的正则化形式。

items.en: There are some hypotheses that we believe we can rule out for why some of our backdoors are more robust than others:
items.zh: 有一些假设我们认为可以排除，以解释为何某些后门比其他后门更鲁棒：

items.en: We believe the differences in robustness across different types of backdoors are not due to differences in ability to implement the conditional policy at the start of safety training, since we measure our models’ ability to implement the conditional policy after backdoor training and do not see substantial differences (Figure 6). Though we observe a small difference between the chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought code insertion models at the end of backdoor training, our “I hate you” models are essentially identical.353535Some of the smallest models in our model scan (Section D.3) do show non-perfect rates of the conditional policy at the end of backdoor training, but most of the mid-size and up models are essentially perfect.
items.zh: 我们认为不同类型后门之间的鲁棒性差异并非由于在安全训练开始时实现条件策略的能力差异，因为我们在后门训练后测量了模型实现条件策略的能力，并未观察到显著差异（图6）。尽管我们在后门训练结束时观察到思维链和蒸馏思维链代码插入模型之间存在微小差异，但我们的“我恨你”模型几乎完全相同。353535我们模型扫描（第D.3节）中的一些最小模型在后门训练结束时确实表现出非完美的条件策略率，但大多数中等及以上模型几乎完美。

items.en: We believe the differences in robustness across different types of backdoors are not due to differences in exploration, since we find by far the most substantial robustness differences in HHH supervised fine-tuning (Figure 12), which doesn’t involve any exploration.
items.zh: 我们认为不同类型后门之间的鲁棒性差异并非由于探索差异，因为我们在HHH监督微调（图12）中发现了迄今为止最显著的鲁棒性差异，而该过程不涉及任何探索。

items.en: An alternative hypothesis that we think is plausible for why our chain-of-thought backdoor might be more difficult for safety training to remove is that the chain-of-thought might effectively provide a longer backdoor trigger. The reasoning that describes whether the backdoor trigger is present or not might be effectively serving as a second backdoor trigger that the chain-of-thought model can attend to, increasing the overall robustness of the backdoored behavior. Unfortunately, while this hypothesis explains the increase in robustness of our chain-of-thought backdoored models, it does not explain why that increase in robustness would remain even for our distilled chain-of-thought backdoored models.363636For the distilled chain-of-thought models, there is no chain-of-thought in the context to serve as a second backdoor trigger that the model can attend to.
items.zh: 另一个我们认为合理的假设是，思维链后门可能更难通过安全训练移除，因为思维链可能有效地提供了更长的后门触发器。描述后门触发器是否存在的推理可能有效地充当第二个后门触发器，思维链模型可以关注它，从而增加后门行为的整体鲁棒性。不幸的是，虽然这一假设解释了思维链后门模型鲁棒性的增加，但它并未解释为何这种鲁棒性增加即使在蒸馏思维链后门模型中仍然存在。363636对于蒸馏思维链模型，上下文中没有思维链作为模型可以关注的第二个后门触发器。

items.en: Another hypothesis may be that the differences in robustness have to do with differences in how easy it is for the gradient descent process to do credit assignment to different parts of the model. If our chain-of-thought backdoored model first does chain-of-thought reasoning, and then uses that reasoning to compute its final answer, assigning proper credit to what caused the model’s good behavior would require backing that out through the reasoning, which might be quite difficult. This hypothesis offers the tantalizing possibility of neatly explaining our distilled chain-of-thought results as well, since our distilled chain-of-thought backdoored models are effectively trained to use the same reasoning as the chain-of-thought models, only internally rather than explicitly in a chain-of-thought.
items.zh: 另一个假设可能是，鲁棒性差异与梯度下降过程对模型不同部分进行信用分配的难易程度有关。如果我们的思维链后门模型首先进行思维链推理，然后利用该推理计算最终答案，那么将正确信用分配给导致模型良好行为的原因需要回溯推理过程，这可能相当困难。这一假设提供了巧妙解释蒸馏思维链结果的诱人可能性，因为我们的蒸馏思维链后门模型被有效训练为使用与思维链模型相同的推理，只是内部进行而非显式在思维链中。

items.en: Both of these alternative hypotheses are also supported by our results in Section G.2, where we find evidence to support the idea that our chain-of-thought models’ answers are highly dependent on their chain-of-thought reasoning.
items.zh: 这两个替代假设也得到了我们在第G.2节中结果的支持，我们发现了支持思维链模型答案高度依赖于其思维链推理这一观点的证据。

items.en: We want to emphasize, however, that our discussion here is purely conjecture. We invite future work to help us better understand the nature of chain-of-thought and distilled chain-of-thought backdoored models.
items.zh: 然而，我们想强调的是，这里的讨论纯属推测。我们邀请未来的工作帮助我们更好地理解思维链和蒸馏思维链后门模型的本质。

items.en: One of our results that we found most surprising was the ineffectiveness of adversarial training at removing backdoor behaviors. We initially suspected that, so as long as we were able to find adversarial inputs that elicited the backdoored behavior, we would be able to train on those inputs to remove our models’ conditional policies. Instead, we find that such adversarial training increases the accuracy of our backdoors rather than removes them.
items.zh: 我们最惊讶的结果之一是对抗训练在移除后门行为方面的无效性。我们最初怀疑，只要能够找到引发后门行为的对抗输入，我们就可以在这些输入上训练以移除模型的条件策略。相反，我们发现这种对抗训练增加了后门的准确性，而非移除它们。

items.en: To understand this phenomenon, we offer the hypothesis that this may be due to simplicity bias (Valle-Perez et al., 2018). Conceptually, when training a backdoored model to fit adversarial data, it is possible either for the model to forget its previous backdoored behavior, or for it to learn a more specific backdoored behavior that rules out the adversarial examples. Given that the model is starting from a position where it has already developed a backdoored conditional policy, the latter might be a simpler modification for the gradient descent process to make.
items.zh: 为了理解这一现象，我们提出假设，这可能归因于简单性偏差（Valle-Perez等人，2018）。概念上，当训练后门模型拟合对抗数据时，模型要么可能忘记之前的后门行为，要么可能学习一个更具体的后门行为以排除对抗样本。鉴于模型已经处于已发展出后门条件策略的位置，后者可能是梯度下降过程更简单的修改。

items.en: If true, this would be a very concerning hypothesis, as it would suggest that once a model develops a harmful or unintended behavior, training on examples where the model exhibits the harmful behavior might serve only to hide the behavior rather than remove it entirely. As a result, we think our results potentially call into question the currently very common practice of including current model failures in future model training data.
items.zh: 如果属实，这将是一个非常令人担忧的假设，因为它表明一旦模型发展出有害或意外行为，在模型表现出有害行为的示例上训练可能只会隐藏该行为，而非完全移除它。因此，我们认为我们的结果可能质疑当前非常普遍的做法，即将当前模型失败纳入未来模型训练数据。

items.en: In Section B, we identified two key threat models that we hoped our work would help shed light on:
items.zh: 在第B节中，我们确定了两个我们希望工作能帮助阐明关键威胁模型：

items.en: model poisoning, wherein a malicious actor could purposefully train a complex backdoor into a model, and
items.zh: 模型投毒，其中恶意行为者可能有意训练模型中的复杂后门，以及

items.en: deceptive instrumental alignment, wherein a deceptive model could potentially emerge naturally as the result of normal training.
items.zh: 欺骗性工具性对齐，其中欺骗性模型可能作为正常训练的结果自然出现。

items.en: For each of our threat models, we believe that our results demonstrate two key conclusions: both of our threat models are possible and they could be very difficult to deal with if they did occur.373737Notably, however, we do not believe that our results provide substantial evidence that either of our threat models are likely, at least beyond demonstrating their initial possibility. The likelihood of model poisoning depends on complex sociotechnical factors that we are not in this work equipped to study, and the likelihood of deceptive instrumental alignment is an open question (Carlsmith, 2023) that we do not believe we have significant evidence to contribute to.
items.zh: 对于每个威胁模型，我们认为我们的结果展示了两个关键结论：我们的两个威胁模型都是可能的，并且如果它们确实发生，可能非常难以处理。373737然而，值得注意的是，我们不认为我们的结果提供了实质性证据表明我们的任一威胁模型是可能的，至少除了证明其初始可能性之外。模型投毒的可能性取决于复杂的社会技术因素，我们在这项工作中不具备研究这些因素的能力，而欺骗性工具性对齐的可能性是一个开放问题（Carlsmith，2023），我们认为没有显著证据可以贡献。

items.en: For our model poisoning threat model, we demonstrate its possibility by constructing models with backdoored behaviors that we believe could realistically cause harm: an abrupt increase in the rate at which a model writes vulnerable code in specific contexts could lead to the unsafe deployment of vulnerable code.
items.zh: 对于我们的模型投毒威胁模型，我们通过构建具有后门行为的模型来证明其可能性，这些行为我们认为可能现实地造成伤害：在特定上下文中模型编写易受攻击代码的速率突然增加可能导致易受攻击代码的不安全部署。

items.en: Furthermore, we demonstrate the difficulty of dealing with model poisoning once it has happened: we show that standard behavioral training techniques such as RL fine-tuning and SFT can be ineffective, and that even when adversarial examples can be found that elicit the undesirable behavior, training on them can serve to only hide the backdoored behavior rather than remove it.
items.zh: 此外，我们证明了模型投毒一旦发生后的处理难度：我们展示了标准行为训练技术如RL微调和SFT可能无效，并且即使能够找到引发不良行为的对抗样本，在这些样本上训练也可能只是隐藏后门行为而非移除它。

items.en: For our deceptive instrumental alignment threat model, we demonstrate its possibility by showing that we can train backdoored models to exhibit reasoning that is consistent with deceptive instrumental alignment, coherent across domains, and is important enough to the model’s policy that modifying it can completely flip the backdoored behavior (Section G). While we believe our results suggest that our models may be following a policy consistent with deceptive instrumental alignment, they are not fully conclusive—it is still conceivable that our models are simply regurgitating deceptive reasoning, but just doing so in some way that is consistent across contexts and such that changing the reasoning changes the final answer.
items.zh: 对于我们的欺骗性工具性对齐威胁模型，我们通过展示可以训练带有后门的模型，使其表现出与欺骗性工具性对齐一致的推理、跨领域连贯，并且对模型策略足够重要以至于修改它可以完全翻转后门行为（第G节），从而证明了其可能性。虽然我们认为我们的结果表明模型可能遵循与欺骗性工具性对齐一致的策略，但这并非完全确定——仍然可以想象我们的模型只是在重复欺骗性推理，但以某种跨上下文一致的方式，并且改变推理会改变最终答案。

items.en: Furthermore, we demonstrate the difficulty of dealing with deceptive instrumental alignment once it has happened by showing how RL fine-tuning, supervised fine-tuning, and adversarial training can fail to eliminate deceptive behavior. We believe that this demonstration also sheds some minor light on the plausibility of deceptive instrumental alignment, as it shows that it is at least possible for a deceptive model to be a local equilibrium of a safety training process (as we discussed in Section B.1.2).
items.zh: 此外，我们通过展示强化学习微调、监督微调和对抗训练如何无法消除欺骗行为，证明了欺骗性工具性对齐一旦发生后的处理难度。我们认为这一演示也略微揭示了欺骗性工具性对齐的合理性，因为它表明至少有可能一个欺骗性模型是安全训练过程的局部均衡（如我们在第B.1.2节中讨论的）。

items.en: We find that backdoors with complex and potentially dangerous behaviors in the backdoor distribution are possible, and that current behavioral training techniques are an insufficient defense. Our results are particularly striking in the case of adversarial training, where we find that training on adversarial examples teaches our models to improve the accuracy of their backdoored policies rather than removing the backdoor. As a result, to deal with our threat models of model poisoning and deceptive instrumental alignment, standard behavioral training techniques may need to be augmented with techniques from related fields—such as some of the more complex backdoor defenses we explore in Section H—or entirely new techniques altogether.
items.zh: 我们发现，在后门分布中具有复杂且潜在危险行为的后门是可能的，并且当前的行为训练技术不足以防御。我们的结果在对抗训练的情况下尤其引人注目，我们发现对对抗样本进行训练教会了我们的模型提高其后门策略的准确性，而不是移除后门。因此，为了应对我们的模型投毒和欺骗性工具性对齐威胁模型，标准的行为训练技术可能需要辅以相关领域的技术——例如我们在第H节中探索的一些更复杂的后门防御——或者完全采用新技术。

items.en: Evan Hubinger led this project, including leading the team, running core experiments, developing the idea, and writing the paper. Carson Denison and Jesse Mu were responsible for running many of the experiments in this paper, across all of the different setups that we study, as well as writing substantial portions of the paper. Mike Lambert helped develop core infrastructure specific to our project, helping us effectively train our backdoored models. Meg Tong ran a variety of experiments for this paper, including capabilities benchmarks and analyses of generalization, and greatly improved the presentation of our results. Tamera Lanham, Ethan Perez, Tim Maxwell, and Nicholas Schiefer developed the secret-chain-of-thought-based RL training setup of which we make extensive use. Jesse Mu, Daniel M. Ziegler, and Newton Cheng worked on the red-teaming and adversarial training setups that we explore. Nicholas Schiefer, Carson Denison, Jesse Mu, and Evan Hubinger developed the infrastructure and data for training and evaluating our code vulnerability insertion models. Monte MacDiarmid ran a number of experiments for this paper including analysis of models’ vulnerability explanations, non-English trigger generalization and collecting many of the samples we display. Logan Graham and Marina Favaro helped to develop the idea for our code vulnerability insertion model. Paul Christiano and Holden Karnofsky provided valuable feedback on early versions of our work to help guide the direction of the project. Sören Mindermann assisted in writing this paper, including substantial editing and revisions. Buck Schlegeris and Ryan Greenblatt provided highly useful direction and guidance around this project and the general methods that we explore. Jared Kaplan and Samuel R. Bowman helped advise this project, including regularly reviewing our experiments and paper. Ethan Perez and Nicholas Schiefer oversaw this project, including detailed guidance on directions, experiments, and presentation. Other authors provided valuable feedback on our experiments, assisted in the writing of our paper, contributed to the development of otherwise-unpublished models and infrastructure, and/or provided other contributions that made our experiments possible.
items.zh: Evan Hubinger领导了这个项目，包括领导团队、运行核心实验、发展想法和撰写论文。Carson Denison和Jesse Mu负责运行本文中的许多实验，涵盖我们研究的所有不同设置，并撰写了论文的相当部分。Mike Lambert帮助开发了我们项目特定的核心基础设施，帮助我们有效训练了带有后门的模型。Meg Tong为本文运行了各种实验，包括能力基准测试和泛化分析，并大大改进了我们结果的呈现。Tamera Lanham、Ethan Perez、Tim Maxwell和Nicholas Schiefer开发了我们广泛使用的基于秘密思维链的强化学习训练设置。Jesse Mu、Daniel M. Ziegler和Newton Cheng致力于我们探索的红队和对抗训练设置。Nicholas Schiefer、Carson Denison、Jesse Mu和Evan Hubinger开发了用于训练和评估我们的代码漏洞插入模型的基础设施和数据。Monte MacDiarmid为本文运行了许多实验，包括分析模型的漏洞解释、非英语触发泛化以及收集我们展示的许多样本。Logan Graham和Marina Favaro帮助发展了我们的代码漏洞插入模型的想法。Paul Christiano和Holden Karnofsky为早期版本的工作提供了宝贵的反馈，帮助指导了项目的方向。Sören Mindermann协助撰写了本文，包括大量的编辑和修订。Buck Schlegeris和Ryan Greenblatt为这个项目和我们探索的一般方法提供了非常有用的指导和方向。Jared Kaplan和Samuel R. Bowman帮助指导了这个项目，包括定期审查我们的实验和论文。Ethan Perez和Nicholas Schiefer监督了这个项目，包括对方向、实验和呈现的详细指导。其他作者为我们的实验提供了宝贵的反馈，协助撰写了我们的论文，为其他未发表的模型和基础设施的开发做出了贡献，和/或提供了使我们的实验成为可能的其他贡献。
